source_idea.paper_title: CODA: Rewriting Transformer Blocks as GEMM-Epilogue Programs
source_idea.read: The applicable lesson is not just to call the sparse dKV body a GEMM epilogue. The useful pattern is to keep MFMA accumulator results on chip, apply cheap tile-local transforms/reductions before global stores, and emit only compact partials for a lightweight auxiliary reducer. If the epilogue itself becomes a dense active-group table, it loses the benefit.

implemented_probe.contract: Same actual-LI pair-slot route metadata and rocWMMA query-major contribution body as compact_pairslot_atomic, but omit final dKV atomic/writeback and instead write a tiny per-CTA sink. This isolates local GEMM-epilogue-style pair-slot accumulation from final global emit.

remote_validation.contaminated_note: The first accum-sink run landed on GPU0 after another workload occupied GPUs0-3 and doubled contrib/raw timings, so it is kept only as contamination evidence. The clean decision uses GPU4 with 3 warmups and 5 timed iterations.

why: The local pair-slot accumulation path is already about 18.85 ms without final global dKV atomics.
The final global emit is effectively not the limiting piece in this topology: compact_pairslot_atomic is about the same as accum_sink.
Raw atomic remains about 10.04 ms, only about 1.01 ms above contrib_sink, so reducing global writes with a dense local table is the wrong tradeoff.
This confirms the GEMM+epilogue abstraction only helps if the epilogue reducer is sparse/segmented at warp/block level, not dense active_group x D.
next_kernel_contract: Keep query-major MFMA contribution production and actual-LI pair-slot metadata.
Replace dense active-group local tables with warp/lane/block segmented reduction over touched pair slots.
Emit compact reduced rows or a small final scatter only after segmented reduction.
If TileLang cannot express that sparse epilogue safely, move this slice to HIP/rocWMMA/CK or rocPRIM-style segmented primitives rather than iterating on dense TileLang tables.
